title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
During legal proceedings regarding the death of Andrew Van, District Attorney Krümmit reveals that Van was an American citizen who previously lived in Pittsburgh, where records identified him as an Armenian born in 1885. Ellery considers the implications of Van's Armenian background. The prosecution also discusses Clinton, Van's servant, who was an orphan raised at St. Vincent's Orphanage in Pittsburgh. The grocer, Bernheim, supports the description of Clinton as a good-natured, exceptionally strong man capable of lifting heavy sugar barrels.

Mayor and banker Matt Hollis testifies that Van was a hardworking, quiet man who rented a house from the Mayor. Hollis notes that Van requested unusual philosophical and historical books from Pittsburgh and kept his salary in cash at home. The sheriff testifies that a search of Van's house on December 25th revealed no money, and the marshal presents an empty, deformed green tin box that Van used to store his funds. Additionally, the postmaster testifies that Van received very little mail and had not sent any letters in several months.

Dr. Strong, the pathologist, testifies that Van likely died around midnight on Christmas Eve from a gunshot wound or a blow to the head. He notes that the body had nail holes in the hands and feet, and that the head was severed with a very sharp tool after death. Coroner Stapleton presents a long-handled axe found in Van's kitchen. The weapon, which belonged to the deceased and was typically used by the missing Clinton, bore no fingerprints, suggesting the killer wore gloves or cleaned the blade.
